summary: DrawerViewModel + DrawerContent implementation is structurally sound: combine(observeChats, registry.models) → DateSection list with correct LocalDate-zone bucketing; events via SharedFlow keep the VM NavController-free; Material3 SwipeToDismissBox + AlertDialog wiring is coherent; hand-rolled fakes follow patterns.md. AC-P4 / AC-U1 / AC-U3 / AC-M1 are all addressed. Findings are optimisation / hardening in nature — no critical blockers. The swipe-to-confirm deviation from the literal task wording is acceptable (AC-U3 intent preserved: confirmation dialog still precedes CASCADE DELETE) and should be logged in decisions.md.

suggestions.suggestion: DeleteChatDialog silently swallows a failed message-count fetch with `runCatching { messageCountProvider() }.getOrDefault(0)`. A Room read failure here is a `history-read` incident per patterns.md (ErrorLog whitelist), not a zero-message chat. Currently the user would see a confirmation dialog claiming '0 сообщ.' immediately before deleting a chat that may have content — mis-informing an irreversible action. Log the failure and/or fall back to the without-count body string.
suggestions.benefit: Surfacing Room failures to the error log preserves the feedback/diagnostics loop (AC for error logging, Phase 2.5 convention) and avoids misleading the user right before CASCADE DELETE. Change to: `runCatching { messageCountProvider() }.onFailure { ErrorLog.e("history-read", "drawer delete-dialog count failed", it) }.getOrNull()` and let the composable fall back to the no-count body when the value is null.

suggestions.suggestion: `renameChat` (blank path) calls `messageDao.getByChatId(chatId)` which loads EVERY message row just to pick the first USER one. For a chat with hundreds of messages this copies a large list through a coroutine boundary on every reset-to-auto-title. Add a dedicated DAO query (e.g. `@Query("SELECT * FROM messages WHERE chat_id = :chatId AND role = :role ORDER BY created_at ASC LIMIT 1") suspend fun firstByChatIdAndRole(chatId: Long, role: String = "user"): MessageEntity?`) and use it here.
suggestions.benefit: O(1) DB access (LIMIT 1) instead of O(N) list copy; also clarifies intent at the call site. This is the same trade-off flagged in the task preamble (no repo-interface expansion) — a DAO-level query keeps the repo narrow while avoiding the N-row cost.

suggestions.suggestion: `checkModelAvailable(chatId)` reads `drawerUiState.value.sections` and then re-derives availability from registry. Two problems: (1) With `SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed(5_000L)`, after 5s with no subscribers the StateFlow resets to `DrawerUiState.Initial` (empty sections) — `checkModelAvailable` then spuriously returns `false` for every chat. (2) `ChatRowUiModel` already carries `isModelAvailable` precomputed, so the second registry lookup is redundant. Currently the UI uses `row.isModelAvailable` directly and never calls `checkModelAvailable` — so this is effectively dead code whose only exerciser is the test suite. Either delete the method (UI has everything it needs on the row) or re-ground it in `registry.models.value` directly (by chat.modelId via chatDao.getById) so it doesn't depend on a hot-cache subscriber window.
suggestions.benefit: Either simpler code (deletion) or correctness under `WhileSubscribed` timeout (re-grounding). As written, a future caller of `checkModelAvailable` after the drawer has been closed for 5+s would get wrong answers.

suggestions.suggestion: `confirmValueChange` imperatively calls `onSwipeDelete()` as a side-effect and then returns `false` to snap the box back. This abuses `confirmValueChange` (it's a predicate, not an event callback) and makes the swipe state fragile — if Material3 changes semantics, the delete dialog could trigger multiple times or not at all. After the dialog is dismissed, the swipe state should be explicitly reset via `LaunchedEffect(pendingDelete == null) { dismissState.reset() }` to guarantee the background red stripe disappears cleanly on Cancel. Also consider calling onSwipeDelete only when `value == EndToStart` AND the previous value was `Settled` to avoid duplicate firings.
suggestions.benefit: Robust against M3 behavioural changes; guarantees the red background + trash icon vanish when the user taps Cancel in the confirmation dialog; cleaner separation of predicate vs action.

suggestions.suggestion: `formatRelative` constructs a new `SimpleDateFormat` per invocation; with N chats this allocates N formatters per `buildSections` call. Hoist two `DateTimeFormatter` instances (one for today-same-day `HH:mm`, one for other-day `d MMM`) into companion objects or pass a pre-built pair through `buildSections`. `DateTimeFormatter` is also thread-safe (unlike `SimpleDateFormat`), making this the idiomatic java.time choice in a codebase that already uses `LocalDate`.
suggestions.benefit: Removes allocation churn on every drawer emission and eliminates the SimpleDateFormat/locale-sensitivity footgun; consistent with the `java.time` stack already adopted in this file.

suggestions.suggestion: `formatRelative` calls `clock()` on every row (L263), while `buildSections` already computes `today` once (L211). Pass `today` into `toRow` / `formatRelative` as a parameter — a single consistent `today` per emission prevents any theoretical day-rollover race between `buildSections` (line 211) and `formatRelative` (line 263) during the same build.
suggestions.benefit: Trivial correctness hardening around midnight boundaries and reduces the number of `LocalDate.now()` clock reads per render from (chats.size + 1) to 1.

suggestions.suggestion: Magic constant `MAX_MANUAL_TITLE_LEN = 60` is declared both here (DrawerViewModel.kt:35) and in DrawerContent.kt:59. Extract to a single `object DrawerConstants` or a companion on `DrawerViewModel` / `ChatRowUiModel` so a change is made in one place. Defensive double-capping (UI caps at entry + VM caps on save) is fine, but the constant itself must not drift.
suggestions.benefit: Prevents the two copies diverging (e.g. a future task changing UI cap to 80 but forgetting the VM, producing a silent truncation at save).

suggestions.suggestion: `arguments?.getLong("chatId")` returns `0L` (not null) when the key is absent, because `Bundle.getLong(key)` never returns null. The outer `takeIf { route == "chat/{chatId}" }` already gates this, so a zero value is only possible if the route itself is wrong — but the implicit 0L-means-no-chat convention is easy to break. Prefer `it.arguments?.let { if (it.containsKey("chatId")) it.getLong("chatId") else null }` for an explicit null signal, or use `savedStateHandle.get<Long>("chatId")` via a typed access helper.
suggestions.benefit: Removes a subtle failure mode where `currentChatId == 0L` could collide with a genuine chat id 0 if the AUTOINCREMENT primary-key assumption ever changes.

suggestions.suggestion: The deviation from the literal task wording (swipe-to-reveal-button → swipe-to-confirm-dialog) is well-documented in the file KDoc, but should also land in `work/phase-3-history/decisions.md` for the task-9 entry so future readers don't need to reverse-engineer it. Task spec's AC-U3 wording ("Swipe → красная кнопка 'Удалить' → confirmation dialog") is still semantically satisfied — the red background + trash icon is the visual affordance, the dialog is the confirmation gate — but making this explicit avoids re-litigation.
suggestions.benefit: Traceability between tech-spec and implementation; makes the intentional deviation auditable.

suggestions.suggestion: The task explicitly asks for boundary cases (midnight, day-transition) in the date-grouping tests. Current tests cover four happy-path buckets with timestamps pinned to `LocalTime.NOON`. Add one test with `lastMessageAt` at 23:59 yesterday local (epoch ms of `today.minusDays(1).atTime(23, 59)`) asserting YESTERDAY, and a test at 00:01 today asserting TODAY. This is the class of bug the tech-spec's 'граница — по датам в локальном часовом поясе, не по 24-часовым интервалам' clause is designed to prevent.
suggestions.benefit: Pins the zone-local boundary semantics; any future refactor that accidentally regresses to a 24h rolling window will fail here instead of surviving to device smoke.

suggestions.suggestion: `ModelChip` uses `Modifier.fillMaxHeight()` inside a `Row(verticalAlignment = CenterVertically)` that wraps content — `fillMaxHeight` on a wrap-content parent resolves to the tallest sibling's height, which in this case is the date Text. This is harmless but confusing; drop `fillMaxHeight()` and let the chip size to its own padding. Also a very long `modelDisplayName` inside the chip will push the relative-date out of view because the chip has no `weight` constraint — consider `.widthIn(max = ...)` or giving the chip a `weight(1f, fill = false)` with `maxLines = 1 + ellipsis` already set.
suggestions.benefit: Predictable layout under long model display names (e.g. full HF id fallback when allowlist misses) without chip overrunning the row.

suggestions.suggestion: `mutableStateOf<Int?>(null)` in Compose triggers boxing of every Int write; use `mutableIntStateOf(-1)` with `-1` as the sentinel for 'not yet loaded'. Trivial perf but idiomatic in modern Compose.
suggestions.benefit: Avoids Int autoboxing on state writes; tiny allocation saving.
